attachment.stdout: {"systemMessage":"\n\nEntire CLI will link this conversation to your next commit."}
attachment.command: sh -c 'if ! command -v entire >/dev/null 2>&1; then printf "%s\n" "{\"systemMessage\":\"\\n\\nEntire CLI is enabled but not installed or not on PATH.\\nInstallation guide: https://docs.entire.io/cli/installation#installation-methods\"}"; exit 0; fi; exec entire hooks claude-code session-start'

attachment.content: Entire CLI will link this conversation to your next commit.

- verify-all: Run the full check + clippy sweep across the workspace AND the WASM plugin (panopt-zellij). Use after non-trivial edits, before declaring work done, or when asked to verify the repo builds cleanly. Captures the dual-target dance (workspace builds for host, panopt-zellij builds only for wasm32-wasip1) so you don't have to remember the right manifest-path/target each time.
- update-config: Use this skill to configure the Claude Code harness via settings.json. Automated behaviors ("from now on when X", "each time X", "whenever X", "before/after X") require hooks configured in settings.json - the harness executes these, not Claude, so memory/preferences cannot fulfill them. Also use for: permissions ("allow X", "add permission", "move permission to"), env vars ("set X=Y"), hook troubleshooting, or any changes to settings.json/settings.local.json files. Examples: "allow npm commands", "add bq permission to global settings", "move permission to user settings", "set DEBUG=true", "when claude stops show X". For simple settings like theme/model, suggest the /config command.
- keybindings-help: Use when the user wants to customize keyboard shortcuts, rebind keys, add chord bindings, or modify ~/.claude/keybindings.json. Examples: "rebind ctrl+s", "add a chord shortcut", "change the submit key", "customize keybindings".
- simplify: Review changed code for reuse, quality, and efficiency, then fix any issues found.
- fewer-permission-prompts: Scan your transcripts for common read-only Bash and MCP tool calls, then add a prioritized allowlist to project .claude/settings.json to reduce permission prompts.
- loop: Run a prompt or slash command on a recurring interval (e.g. /loop 5m /foo). Omit the interval to let the model self-pace. - When the user wants to set up a recurring task, poll for status, or run something repeatedly on an interval (e.g. "check the deploy every 5 minutes", "keep running /babysit-prs"). Do NOT invoke for one-off tasks.
- schedule: Create, update, list, or run scheduled remote agents (routines) that execute on a cron schedule. - When the user wants to schedule a recurring remote agent, set up automated tasks, create a cron job for Claude Code, or manage their scheduled agents/routines. Also use when the user wants a one-time scheduled run ("run this once at 3pm", "remind me to check X tomorrow").
- claude-api: Build, debug, and optimize Claude API / Anthropic SDK apps. Apps built with this skill should include prompt caching. Also handles migrating existing Claude API code between Claude model versions (4.5 → 4.6, 4.6 → 4.7, retired-model replacements).
TRIGGER when: code imports `anthropic`/`@anthropic-ai/sdk`; user asks for the Claude API, Anthropic SDK, or Managed Agents; user adds/modifies/tunes a Claude feature (caching, thinking, compaction, tool use, batch, files, citations, memory) or model (Opus/Sonnet/Haiku) in a file; questions about prompt caching / cache hit rate in an Anthropic SDK project.
SKIP: file imports `openai`/other-provider SDK, filename like `*-openai.py`/`*-generic.py`, provider-neutral code, general programming/ML.
- init: Initialize a new CLAUDE.md file with codebase documentation
- review: Review a pull request
- security-review: Complete a security review of the pending changes on the current branch

content.text: I'll explore the search popup implementation to understand the Esc handling.

input.prompt:
I'm investigating a bug in the panopt-zellij plugin at /home/gregz/cp/panopt/crates/panopt-zellij/. Recent commit aef4d3d added a "Cockpit-wide search popup (#33)". The bug: when the search popup is opened from certain sidebar panes (Agents, Terminals, Commands, Scratchpads), pressing Esc does NOT close the search pane/popup. When opened from other panes (presumably Todos), Esc works fine.

I need to understand:

1. Where the search popup/pane is implemented in the panopt-zellij plugin. Find the file(s) and the function(s) that render and manage the search popup.
2. How Esc key input is handled by the plugin overall — what's the event handler? Where is Key::Esc matched?
3. How focus / which-sidebar-pane state is tracked. Specifically what distinguishes the "Agents", "Terminals", "Commands", "Scratchpads" sidebar panes from the "Todos" pane in the code.
4. Where the search popup is opened from each of those sidebar contexts — find every call site that opens the search popup, and any per-context divergence in how Esc gets handled (e.g., a key handler short-circuits before Esc reaches the search-close logic).
5. The likely root cause: a missing/early-return case in the per-pane key handler, or a focus-tracking issue where the plugin doesn't know the popup is open when those panes are active. Trace the actual control flow if you can.

Please return:
- The exact file paths (with line numbers) for the search popup rendering, the central key dispatch, and the per-sidebar-pane key handlers.
- A short trace of how Esc is handled in the WORKING case (e.g., from Todos pane) vs the BROKEN case (e.g., from Agents pane).
- Your best diagnosis of why Esc fails for those four panes.

Keep the report tight — under 500 words. Use file_path:line_number references.

content.text:
Perfect. Now I have the full picture. Let me write the report:

---

## Bug Root Cause Analysis: Search Popup Esc Key Not Closing in Non-Todos Panes

### File Locations & Key Code Points

**Search popup implementation:**
- `/home/gregz/cp/panopt/crates/panopt/src/search.rs:170` - Esc key handler that sends "panopt:close-search" pipe
- `/home/gregz/cp/panopt/crates/panopt/src/search.rs:376` - `send_close()` function pipes message

**Sidebar plugin main dispatch:**
- `/home/gregz/cp/panopt/crates/panopt-zellij/src/main.rs:1464-1556` - `handle_key()` function where Alt-/ opens search from any pane
- `/home/gregz/cp/panopt/crates/panopt-zellij/src/main.rs:679-739` - `pipe()` function with mode guard
- `/home/gregz/cp/panopt/crates/panopt-zellij/src/main.rs:725-735` - Search pipe handlers (open/show-result/close-search)

**Zellij configuration:**
- `/home/gregz/cp/panopt/crates/panopt/src/up.rs:130` - SEARCH_BIND_TO with `--plugin-configuration "mode=todos"`

### Control Flow Comparison

**WORKING (Todos pane):**
1. User presses Alt-/ in Todos sidebar pane
2. Zellij keybind (up.rs:130) pipes `panopt:open-search` with `mode=todos`
3. Todos plugin instance receives pipe → `spawn_search_dialog()` (main.rs:726)
4. User presses Esc in search TUI
5. search.rs:170 matches Esc → calls `send_close()` → pipes `panopt:close-search` with `mode=todos`
6. Todos plugin receives close-search pipe → `close_search_pane()` (main.rs:734)
7. **Search closes successfully**

**BROKEN (Agents/Terminals/Commands/Scratchpads panes):**
1. User presses Alt-/ in (e.g.) Agents sidebar pane
2. Alt-/ is NOT intercepted by Zellij keybind (happens in locked panes only) — instead handled by Agents plugin's `handle_key()` (main.rs:1549-1550)
3. Agents plugin calls `spawn_search_dialog()` directly (main.rs:1550)
4. **search_pane is now tracked in the Agents plugin instance**
5. User presses Esc in search TUI
6. search.rs:170 matches Esc → pipes `panopt:close-search` with `mode=todos`
7. Plugin dispatch `pipe()` at main.rs:680 **rejects the message** because `self.mode != Mode::Todos` — returns false
8. **Agents plugin instance never receives close-search**
9. **Search pane stays open**

### Root Cause

The pipe dispatch at **main.rs:680** gates ALL pipe messages on `if self.mode != Mode::Todos { return false; }`. This was correct for Todos-only operations (spawn-agent, spawn-blank-pane, quit-request, etc.), but the search pipes (open-search, show-result, close-search) are now initiated from any sidebar pane, not just Todos.

The search.rs CLI hardcodes `--plugin-configuration "mode=todos"` (search.rs:387) under the assumption the Todos pane always owns the search popup lifecycle. But when a non-Todos pane calls `spawn_search_dialog()` directly (via handle_key), that pane stores the search_pane ID in its own instance, and then the close-search pipe never reaches it.

### Fix Required

Remove the `if self.mode != Mode::Todos` guard from the search pipe handlers (lines 725-736), or move those three pipes outside the guard. The other pipes (spawn-agent, quit-request, etc.) correctly need the guard, but search pipes must be accessible from any mode.

content.text: I have enough to design the fix. Let me write the plan.

input.content:
# Plan: Esc closes search popup regardless of which sidebar opened it (#104)

## Context

The cockpit-wide search popup (added in aef4d3d, "Cockpit-wide search popup (#33)") closes correctly on Esc when opened from the Todos sidebar pane or from a locked content pane, but fails to close when opened from the Agents, Terminals, Commands, or Scratchpads sidebar panes. The user sees the popup stay on screen until they manually close the pane.

### Root cause

Five instances of the `panopt-zellij` plugin run simultaneously, one per sidebar pane. Each instance has its own `search_pane: Option<PaneId>` field (`crates/panopt-zellij/src/main.rs:421`). There are two paths that open the search popup:

1. **Locked content pane** — Zellij keybind defined in `crates/panopt/src/up.rs:130` fires `zellij action pipe --name panopt:open-search --plugin-configuration "mode=todos"`. Zellij delivers the pipe only to the Todos plugin instance, which calls `spawn_search_dialog()` (handled in `pipe()` at `main.rs:725-728`). `search_pane` is stored on the **Todos instance**.

2. **Sidebar plugin pane** — `handle_key()` at `main.rs:1549-1551` matches `Alt-/` on the currently-focused sidebar instance and calls `self.spawn_search_dialog()` **directly**. `search_pane` is stored on **that instance** (Agents/Terminals/Commands/Scratchpads when the user is focused there).

When the user presses Esc inside the popup, `panopt search` runs `zellij action pipe --name panopt:close-search --plugin-configuration "mode=todos"` (`crates/panopt/src/search.rs:376-388`). Zellij delivers it only to the Todos instance. In case (2), the Todos instance's `search_pane` is `None`, so `close_search_pane()` is a no-op — meanwhile the actual `search_pane` lives on a different instance that never receives the pipe.

This violates the invariant documented at `main.rs:674-678`: "Only the Todos pane handles the cockpit-wide pipes." The `handle_key` Alt-/ arm slipped past that invariant.

The same defect causes a second (latent) bug: when a search opened from a non-Todos sidebar pane is dismissed via **Enter** (selection), `panopt:show-result` is also routed via `--plugin-configuration "mode=todos"` — so the Todos instance opens the document but the non-Todos instance's `search_pane` is never cleared, leaving an orphan pane reference. Fixing the root cause fixes both.

### Intended outcome

Esc (and Enter) in the search popup closes it regardless of which sidebar pane had focus when the user pressed `Alt-/`. The Todos instance becomes the canonical owner of the search popup in all paths.

## Approach

Route the `Alt-/` keypress through the existing pipe-to-Todos mechanism instead of letting each plugin instance spawn its own search popup. After the fix, both paths (locked content pane keybind and sidebar plugin pane keypress) flow through the same `panopt:open-search` pipe handler on the Todos instance, restoring the documented invariant.

Use Zellij's in-plugin `pipe_message_to_plugin` API (`zellij-tile::shim::pipe_message_to_plugin`, available via `zellij_tile::prelude::*` which `main.rs:36` already imports). Filtering by `plugin_config = {"mode": "todos"}` selects the existing Todos instance, mirroring the CLI-side `--plugin-configuration "mode=todos"` filter used elsewhere.

### Critical files

- **`crates/panopt-zellij/src/main.rs`** (the only file edited)
  - Replace the body of the `Alt-/` arm at `main.rs:1549-1551` so it sends a `panopt:open-search` `MessageToPlugin` to the Todos instance instead of calling `self.spawn_search_dialog()` directly. The Todos instance's existing `pipe()` arm at `main.rs:725-728` already invokes `spawn_search_dialog()`; that becomes the sole entry point. When the focused pane *is* the Todos sidebar pane, the message routes back to self — `spawn_search_dialog()`'s `if self.search_pane.is_some() { return; }` guard at `main.rs:1715-1717` keeps it idempotent.
  - Update the rustdoc comment above the arm (currently `main.rs:1543-1548`) to reflect that the sidebar path now forwards to the Todos instance rather than spawning locally.

### Sketch

```rust
// crates/panopt-zellij/src/main.rs, replacing lines 1543-1551
// `Alt-/` opens the cockpit's popup search. Both the Zellij-level
// keybind (see up::SEARCH_BIND_TO) and this arm forward to the Todos
// plugin instance, which is the canonical owner of the popup's
// lifecycle (see the invariant comment on `pipe()`). Routing through
// the Todos instance is what makes Esc / Enter close the popup
// regardless of which sidebar pane had focus.
BareKey::Char('/') if key.key_modifiers.contains(&KeyModifier::Alt) => {
    pipe_message_to_plugin(
        MessageToPlugin::new("panopt:open-search")
            .with_plugin_config(BTreeMap::from([(
                "mode".to_string(),
                "todos".to_string(),
            )])),
    );
}
BareKey::Char('/') => {}
```

`BTreeMap` is already in scope (`main.rs` uses it elsewhere, e.g. `main.rs:1733`).

### Why this approach over the alternative

The alternative would be to widen delivery on the *close* side — drop `--plugin-configuration "mode=todos"` from `crates/panopt/src/search.rs:386-387` so every instance sees `close-search` and `show-result`, and add idempotency guards. That works for `close-search` (already idempotent) but `handle_search_result` calls `open_document` (`main.rs:1771-1772`), which is **not** idempotent — five instances would each try to open the chosen document. Guarding it requires each instance to gate on `self.search_pane.is_some()`, which adds a second source of truth for popup ownership. Centralizing on the Todos instance keeps the invariant single-source.

## Verification

1. Rebuild the plugin: `just plugin-release` (or the equivalent `cargo build --manifest-path crates/panopt-zellij/Cargo.toml --target wasm32-wasip1 --release`).
2. Full refresh so the daemon and Zellij session pick up the new wasm artifact: `just refresh`.
3. In the cockpit, repro the bug *before* trusting the fix is wired up:
   - Focus the **Agents** sidebar pane → `Alt-/` → confirm popup opens → Esc → popup closes.
   - Repeat for **Terminals**, **Commands**, **Scratchpads** sidebar panes.
   - Regression check: focus **Todos** sidebar pane → `Alt-/` → Esc → popup closes (path that already worked).
   - Regression check: focus a **locked content pane** (any viewer) → `Alt-/` → Esc → popup closes (path that already worked via the Zellij keybind).
4. Also verify selection paths from each origin: open popup from Agents pane → arrow-down → Enter → confirm the selected document opens in the viewer slot **and** no orphan floating pane lingers.
5. Static checks: `just check` and `just clippy` (each sweeps the workspace and the wasm plugin).

content.content: File created successfully at: /home/gregz/.claude/plans/plan-104-indexed-diffie.md (file state is current in your context — no need to Read it back)

### Sketch

```rust
// crates/panopt-zellij/src/main.rs, replacing lines 1543-1551
// `Alt-/` opens the cockpit's popup search. Both the Zellij-level
// keybind (see up::SEARCH_BIND_TO) and this arm forward to the Todos
// plugin instance, which is the canonical owner of the popup's
// lifecycle (see the invariant comment on `pipe()`). Routing through
// the Todos instance is what makes Esc / Enter close the popup
// regardless of which sidebar pane had focus.
BareKey::Char('/') if key.key_modifiers.contains(&KeyModifier::Alt) => {
    pipe_message_to_plugin(
        MessageToPlugin::new("panopt:open-search")
            .with_plugin_config(BTreeMap::from([(
                "mode".to_string(),
                "todos".to_string(),
            )])),
    );
}
BareKey::Char('/') => {}
```

`BTreeMap` is already in scope (`main.rs` uses it elsewhere, e.g. `main.rs:1733`).

### Why this approach over the alternative

The alternative would be to widen delivery on the *close* side — drop `--plugin-configuration "mode=todos"` from `crates/panopt/src/search.rs:386-387` so every instance sees `close-search` and `show-result`, and add idempotency guards. That works for `close-search` (already idempotent) but `handle_search_result` calls `open_document` (`main.rs:1771-1772`), which is **not** idempotent — five instances would each try to open the chosen document. Guarding it requires each instance to gate on `self.search_pane.is_some()`, which adds a second source of truth for popup ownership. Centralizing on the Todos instance keeps the invariant single-source.

## Verification

1. Rebuild the plugin: `just plugin-release` (or the equivalent `cargo build --manifest-path crates/panopt-zellij/Cargo.toml --target wasm32-wasip1 --release`).
2. Full refresh so the daemon and Zellij session pick up the new wasm artifact: `just refresh`.
3. In the cockpit, repro the bug *before* trusting the fix is wired up:
   - Focus the **Agents** sidebar pane → `Alt-/` → confirm popup opens → Esc → popup closes.
   - Repeat for **Terminals**, **Commands**, **Scratchpads** sidebar panes.
   - Regression check: focus **Todos** sidebar pane → `Alt-/` → Esc → popup closes (path that already worked).
   - Regression check: focus a **locked content pane** (any viewer) → `Alt-/` → Esc → popup closes (path that already worked via the Zellij keybind).
4. Also verify selection paths from each origin: open popup from Agents pane → arrow-down → Enter → confirm the selected document opens in the viewer slot **and** no orphan floating pane lingers.
5. Static checks: `just check` and `just clippy` (each sweeps the workspace and the wasm plugin).

input.plan:
# Plan: Esc closes search popup regardless of which sidebar opened it (#104)

## Context

The cockpit-wide search popup (added in aef4d3d, "Cockpit-wide search popup (#33)") closes correctly on Esc when opened from the Todos sidebar pane or from a locked content pane, but fails to close when opened from the Agents, Terminals, Commands, or Scratchpads sidebar panes. The user sees the popup stay on screen until they manually close the pane.

### Root cause

Five instances of the `panopt-zellij` plugin run simultaneously, one per sidebar pane. Each instance has its own `search_pane: Option<PaneId>` field (`crates/panopt-zellij/src/main.rs:421`). There are two paths that open the search popup:

1. **Locked content pane** — Zellij keybind defined in `crates/panopt/src/up.rs:130` fires `zellij action pipe --name panopt:open-search --plugin-configuration "mode=todos"`. Zellij delivers the pipe only to the Todos plugin instance, which calls `spawn_search_dialog()` (handled in `pipe()` at `main.rs:725-728`). `search_pane` is stored on the **Todos instance**.

2. **Sidebar plugin pane** — `handle_key()` at `main.rs:1549-1551` matches `Alt-/` on the currently-focused sidebar instance and calls `self.spawn_search_dialog()` **directly**. `search_pane` is stored on **that instance** (Agents/Terminals/Commands/Scratchpads when the user is focused there).

When the user presses Esc inside the popup, `panopt search` runs `zellij action pipe --name panopt:close-search --plugin-configuration "mode=todos"` (`crates/panopt/src/search.rs:376-388`). Zellij delivers it only to the Todos instance. In case (2), the Todos instance's `search_pane` is `None`, so `close_search_pane()` is a no-op — meanwhile the actual `search_pane` lives on a different instance that never receives the pipe.

This violates the invariant documented at `main.rs:674-678`: "Only the Todos pane handles the cockpit-wide pipes." The `handle_key` Alt-/ arm slipped past that invariant.

The same defect causes a second (latent) bug: when a search opened from a non-Todos sidebar pane is dismissed via **Enter** (selection), `panopt:show-result` is also routed via `--plugin-configuration "mode=todos"` — so the Todos instance opens the document but the non-Todos instance's `search_pane` is never cleared, leaving an orphan pane reference. Fixing the root cause fixes both.

### Intended outcome

Esc (and Enter) in the search popup closes it regardless of which sidebar pane had focus when the user pressed `Alt-/`. The Todos instance becomes the canonical owner of the search popup in all paths.

## Approach

Route the `Alt-/` keypress through the existing pipe-to-Todos mechanism instead of letting each plugin instance spawn its own search popup. After the fix, both paths (locked content pane keybind and sidebar plugin pane keypress) flow through the same `panopt:open-search` pipe handler on the Todos instance, restoring the documented invariant.

Use Zellij's in-plugin `pipe_message_to_plugin` API (`zellij-tile::shim::pipe_message_to_plugin`, available via `zellij_tile::prelude::*` which `main.rs:36` already imports). Filtering by `plugin_config = {"mode": "todos"}` selects the existing Todos instance, mirroring the CLI-side `--plugin-configuration "mode=todos"` filter used elsewhere.

### Critical files

- **`crates/panopt-zellij/src/main.rs`** (the only file edited)
  - Replace the body of the `Alt-/` arm at `main.rs:1549-1551` so it sends a `panopt:open-search` `MessageToPlugin` to the Todos instance instead of calling `self.spawn_search_dialog()` directly. The Todos instance's existing `pipe()` arm at `main.rs:725-728` already invokes `spawn_search_dialog()`; that becomes the sole entry point. When the focused pane *is* the Todos sidebar pane, the message routes back to self — `spawn_search_dialog()`'s `if self.search_pane.is_some() { return; }` guard at `main.rs:1715-1717` keeps it idempotent.
  - Update the rustdoc comment above the arm (currently `main.rs:1543-1548`) to reflect that the sidebar path now forwards to the Todos instance rather than spawning locally.

### Sketch

```rust
// crates/panopt-zellij/src/main.rs, replacing lines 1543-1551
// `Alt-/` opens the cockpit's popup search. Both the Zellij-level
// keybind (see up::SEARCH_BIND_TO) and this arm forward to the Todos
// plugin instance, which is the canonical owner of the popup's
// lifecycle (see the invariant comment on `pipe()`). Routing through
// the Todos instance is what makes Esc / Enter close the popup
// regardless of which sidebar pane had focus.
BareKey::Char('/') if key.key_modifiers.contains(&KeyModifier::Alt) => {
    pipe_message_to_plugin(
        MessageToPlugin::new("panopt:open-search")
            .with_plugin_config(BTreeMap::from([(
                "mode".to_string(),
                "todos".to_string(),
            )])),
    );
}
BareKey::Char('/') => {}
```

`BTreeMap` is already in scope (`main.rs` uses it elsewhere, e.g. `main.rs:1733`).

### Why this approach over the alternative

The alternative would be to widen delivery on the *close* side — drop `--plugin-configuration "mode=todos"` from `crates/panopt/src/search.rs:386-387` so every instance sees `close-search` and `show-result`, and add idempotency guards. That works for `close-search` (already idempotent) but `handle_search_result` calls `open_document` (`main.rs:1771-1772`), which is **not** idempotent — five instances would each try to open the chosen document. Guarding it requires each instance to gate on `self.search_pane.is_some()`, which adds a second source of truth for popup ownership. Centralizing on the Todos instance keeps the invariant single-source.

## Verification

1. Rebuild the plugin: `just plugin-release` (or the equivalent `cargo build --manifest-path crates/panopt-zellij/Cargo.toml --target wasm32-wasip1 --release`).
2. Full refresh so the daemon and Zellij session pick up the new wasm artifact: `just refresh`.
3. In the cockpit, repro the bug *before* trusting the fix is wired up:
   - Focus the **Agents** sidebar pane → `Alt-/` → confirm popup opens → Esc → popup closes.
   - Repeat for **Terminals**, **Commands**, **Scratchpads** sidebar panes.
   - Regression check: focus **Todos** sidebar pane → `Alt-/` → Esc → popup closes (path that already worked).
   - Regression check: focus a **locked content pane** (any viewer) → `Alt-/` → Esc → popup closes (path that already worked via the Zellij keybind).
4. Also verify selection paths from each origin: open popup from Agents pane → arrow-down → Enter → confirm the selected document opens in the viewer slot **and** no orphan floating pane lingers.
5. Static checks: `just check` and `just clippy` (each sweeps the workspace and the wasm plugin).

content.content:
refresh:
    #!/usr/bin/env bash
    set -euo pipefail
    if [ -n "${ZELLIJ:-}" ]; then
        echo "run \`just refresh\` from a plain shell, not inside Zellij" >&2
        exit 1
    fi
    # `grep` exits 1 when no panopt-* sessions match - that's a clean slate,
    # not an error. Wrap so `set -o pipefail` doesn't abort the recipe when

content.text:
Edit landed in `crates/panopt-zellij/src/main.rs:1543-1561`. Release wasm rebuilt; `cargo check` and `clippy -D warnings` both clean.

To pick up the new plugin in the live cockpit, run `just refresh` from a plain shell outside Zellij — then verify Alt-/ → Esc closes the popup from Agents, Terminals, Commands, Scratchpads (and that Todos / locked-pane paths still work). I left #104 in_progress; `mac` once you've confirmed.

content.content:
diff --git a/crates/panopt-zellij/src/main.rs b/crates/panopt-zellij/src/main.rs
index 46f2a3c..17331bb 100644
--- a/crates/panopt-zellij/src/main.rs
+++ b/crates/panopt-zellij/src/main.rs
@@ -1540,14 +1540,23 @@ impl PanoptPane {
             // the match so `?` falls through the dismissal path on a second
             // press.
             BareKey::Char('?') => self.show_help = true,
-            // `Alt-/` opens the cockpit's popup search. The same gesture is
-            // also bound at the Zellij level for locked content panes (see
-            // up::SEARCH_BIND_TO); this arm covers focus on a sidebar plugin
-            // pane, where Zellij delivers the key directly to the plugin.
+            // `Alt-/` opens the cockpit's popup search. Both the Zellij-level
+            // keybind for locked content panes (see up::SEARCH_BIND_TO) and
+            // this sidebar-pane arm forward to the Todos plugin instance,
+            // which is the canonical owner of the popup's lifecycle (see the
+            // invariant comment on `pipe()`). Spawning the popup locally on
+            // a non-Todos instance would store `search_pane` on the wrong
+            // instance, leaving the `panopt:close-search` / `panopt:show-
+            // result` pipes - both filtered to `mode=todos` - unable to
+            // reach the holder, so Esc and Enter would silently no-op.
             // Bare `/` is left as a no-op stub so it can still reach an
             // inner program if the user's mental model expects that.
             BareKey::Char('/') if key.key_modifiers.contains(&KeyModifier::Alt) => {
-                self.spawn_search_dialog()
+                pipe_message_to_plugin(
+                    MessageToPlugin::new("panopt:open-search").with_plugin_config(BTreeMap::from(
+                        [("mode".to_string(), "todos".to_string())],
+                    )),
+                );
             }
             BareKey::Char('/') => {}
             _ => return false,

content.content:
aef4d3d Cockpit-wide search popup (#33), locked-block consolidation (#98), todos.md updated timestamp (#99)
ccb8975 Restore auto-copy on drag release in both forms
755295b Stop auto-copying on drag release; Ctrl-Shift-C copies everywhere
faf2b8a Write OSC 52 to the Zellij client's pty fd via /proc, not stdout
34f2e2c Emit OSC 52 from copy_command to stdout instead of /dev/tty
26eb521 Instrument the copy chain so we can see which layer drops the payload
d45cbd3 Wire Zellij's copy_command to an OSC 52 -> /dev/tty helper script
582c547 Run every clipboard path, not just the first to claim success
1ab3265 Drag-to-select inside single-line fields (Title, Tags, Assignee)
e84acb2 Pre-grant WriteToClipboard, then route clipboard through the plugin

input.command:
git add crates/panopt-zellij/src/main.rs && git commit -m "$(cat <<'EOF'
Route Alt-/ from sidebar panes through the Todos instance (#104)

Each of the five sidebar plugin instances tracks its own search_pane
field, but `panopt search` pipes close-search/show-result back with
--plugin-configuration mode=todos, so only the Todos instance ever
receives them. When Alt-/ on a non-Todos sidebar pane spawned the
popup locally, the search_pane handle landed on the wrong instance
and Esc/Enter silently no-op'd.

Forward the keypress to the Todos instance via pipe_message_to_plugin
so the popup's lifecycle stays single-source, matching the
locked-content-pane keybind path and the invariant documented on
pipe().
EOF
)" && git status
